description: Changing the property from vacant to Campground so we can build the infrastructure, roads, plumbing, electrical and outbuildings required for the RV Park.

The project consists of installing a rainwater harvesting system at the New Orleans Ernest N. Morial Convention Center to collect, filter, store, and reuse rainwater from the roof for non-potable applications. The system includes new piping, a pump room with a booster pump, large storage above ground cistern (850,000 gal), and a bridge spanning from MCCNO to the current Lot J above Henderson street. It is designed to reduce stormwater runoff, promote sustainability, and support the facility’s resilience goals

The project consists of a new detached 1-story building to house a constellation program on an existing school campus. It will house students with low incidence disabilities for grades Pre-K 4 through 4th grade. the building will have 10 new classrooms along with supporting infrastructure of restrooms, sensory rooms, therapy rooms, and administration area. The building will have modular building components built off-site and then cladding of brick and fiber cement panels, final roofing, and foundation built on site. Also, inclusive of this work will be a new car parking lot, a bus facility parking lot (ALTERNATES 1 & 2) , an artificial turf soccer field (ALTERNATE 5) and associated landscaping and fencing. PHASED RELEASE FOR FOUNDATION AND UNDERGROUND MEP IS RELEASED AS OF 6-9-25 PER CBO

description: THE PROJECT SCOPE CONSISTS OF THE INTERIOR REMODEL TO AN EXISTING 28,511 SF WHOLE FOODS MARKET LOCATED AT 300 N BROAD STREET IN NEW ORLEANS, LA. THE PROJECT INCLUDES BUT IS NOT LIMITED TO THE MODIFICATION OF EXISTING CHECKOUT EQUIPMENT, FOOD SERVICE EQUIPMENT, AND RETAIL SHELVING AS INDICATED WITHIN THESE DOCUMENTS. THE PROJECT WILL ALSO REQUIRE ELECTRICAL, PLUMBING, AND REFRIGERATION SYSTEMS TO BE MODIFIED TO ACCOMMODATE AND SERVE NEW EQUIPMENT AND PLAN LAYOUT. THERE WILL BE NO CHANGE IN OCCUPANCY, USE, EXITS OR TRAVEL DISTANCE.

Partial interior renovation of historic (circa 1918) masonry building at Tulane University containing offices, classrooms and related support spaces, as described on the contract documents. Project scope includes full renovation of 1st & 4th floors in their entirety, including replacement of all mechanical, electrical, plumbing and fire protection systems on those floors. Project also includes limited (minor) interior alterations to Basement, 2nd & 3rd floors, a new elevator serving all five floors, upgrades to 2-hour rated stair enclosures at three existing stairwells, and two new light monitors at roof to provide daylight to 4th floor spaces. Project includes limited site work associated with the installation of underground utilities. The project does not include any exterior facade repairs or improvements. All work to be done as per plans. *Follow up with SWB for Approval
